highlights.summary: Exclude temp target presets from the main chart and re-filter fetched results with a fresh "now". The NSPredicate.tempTargetsForMainChart predicate was changed to require isPreset == false for scheduled items (Model/Helper/TempTargetStored+Helper.swift). A new TempTargetStored.isVisibleInChart computed property was added to represent which temp targets should be drawn (running or non-preset future scheduled items). Home.StateModel.updateTempTargetsFromController now filters the controller's fetchedObjects with .isVisibleInChart before assigning to the chart model. Tests were added to verify the predicate behavior and chart visibility rules.
highlights.userFacingImpact: Users should no longer see newly saved temp-target presets (never activated) appear in the main chart. Cancelled/expired temp targets that previously remained drawn until the app was backgrounded should also stop being shown because the fetched results are re-filtered with a fresh date before reaching the chart. The change is limited to chart visibility logic for temp targets and adds tests to cover the behavior.

items.summary: Bump APP_DEV_VERSION in Config.xcconfig from 0.8.4.22 to 0.8.4.23 (diff shows the line change) (diff:0433b9eabea7, file:1ac055b51224, commit:384a0dca53f5).
items.userFacingImpact: No functional or user-facing behavior change is evident in this commit; it updates a developer/build version constant (APP_DEV_VERSION) in Config.xcconfig (diff:0433b9eabea7, file:1ac055b51224) and the commit message indicates a CI/dev-version bump (commit:384a0dca53f5). If a deeper impact analysis is required (e.g., how this version string is surfaced in releases or CI flows), human review is recommended.

includedPullRequests.title: Fix temp target presets showing up in the main chart
includedPullRequests.body:
Fixes #1324

Saving a new Temp Target as a preset made it show up in the main chart even though it was never activated. The chart's fetch predicate freezes its date anchors when it is built and only re-anchors when the app comes to the foreground, so a fresh preset (date now, enabled false) matched the clause meant for scheduled temp targets. The same stale anchor also kept a cancelled temp target's full bar on the chart until the app was backgrounded.

Presets are now excluded from the scheduled clause, and the fetched results are filtered again with a fresh date before they reach the chart. Added tests covering the predicate and the visibility rules.
